name: Ross Dependency (New Zealand)

colors.symbolism: the field — the snow and ice of Antarctica
details of the New Zealand Antarctic emblem

description: Variant flag for the Ross Dependency, the New Zealand-claimed sector of Antarctica, often shown as the New Zealand flag with a defacement or as a special design featuring the territory's coat of arms.
history: The Ross Dependency is the area of Antarctica claimed by New Zealand (south of 60°S between 160°E and 150°W), administered since 1923. New Zealand's Antarctic claims are managed in accordance with the 1959 Antarctic Treaty. There is no single formally adopted flag — common usage includes a coat of arms of the Ross Dependency and variants of the New Zealand flag.

features.symbolism: Ross Dependency flag based on New Zealand symbols and Antarctic motifs
